detail.overview: <p>Are you passionate about sustainable agriculture and eager to apply your classroom knowledge in the real world? We’re offering a dynamic field attachment designed to help you grow into a confident, practical, and community-oriented agronomist — with a special focus on organic farming.</p> <p>This is your chance to join a supportive team working directly with farmers, learning how to apply innovative organic farm inputs like our solid & liquid organic fertilizers on real farms across Kenya.</p> <h4>About you</h4> <ul> <li>Final-year Diploma or Degree students pursuing: <ul> <li>Agronomy</li> <li>Crop Production</li> <li>Organic Agriculture</li> <li>Agricultural Extension</li> <li>or other related agricultural fields</li> </ul> </li> <li>Applicants must: <ul> <li>Be available for a continuous 3-month attachment</li> <li>Present a formal recommendation letter from your institution</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p><strong>Strong Added Advantages</strong></p> <ul> <li>Training or certification in Organic Agriculture / Agroecology (KOAN, IFOAM, or other recognized institutions)</li> <li>Evidence of a school-based or personal demo plot, garden, or organic farm experiment</li> <li>Authentic content creation ability, including:</li> </ul> <h4>Responsibilities</h4> <ul> <li>Taking clear farm photos or short videos</li> <li>Documenting demo plots, farmer experiences, or trainings</li> <li>Basic storytelling for social media, reports, or field updates (No perfection required—authentic, honest field content is valued)</li> <li>Interest and confidence in working with agrovets, including: <ul> <li>Supporting agrovet attendants with product knowledge</li> <li>Farmer referrals through agrovet shops</li> <li>In-store demonstrations and farmer education</li> <li>Business development or sales exposure, such as:</li> </ul> </li> <li>Farmer engagement and persuasion</li> <li>Basic marketing or selling experience</li> <li>Ability to explain agricultural products clearly and confidently</li> </ul>

detail.category: Any Field of Study
detail.overview: <p><strong>Launch your career with KRA’s Industrial Attachment Programme!</strong> Are you a driven 3rd, 4th, or final year undergraduate or final year diploma student? Gain invaluable practical experience in your field through KRA’s 3-month Industrial Attachment Programme. This is your chance to develop your skills in a wide range of disciplines within KRA’s Support Services departments. <strong>Don’t miss out!</strong></p> <hr /> <p>In support of the Government’s Youth Initiative to develop a pool of young talent for the Kenyan Labour Market, KRA is seeking applications from <strong>continuing 1<sup>st</sup> degree students (Undergraduate)</strong> in their 3<sup>rd</sup>, 4<sup>th</sup> and final year of study and <strong>Diploma students </strong>in their final year for its three (3) months <strong>(May to July 2026) </strong>Industrial Attachment Programme.</p> <p>The programme aims at providing the Youth an opportunity to acquire practical aspects of their respective areas of specialisation in partial fulfilment of their degree programme. The opportunities are open within the various Support Services departments of the Authority in the following disciplines: Finance, Accounting, Economics, Statistics, Data Analytics, Mathematics, Supply Chain Management, Human Resource Management, Marketing & Communication, Communications, International Relations, Diplomacy, Public Relations, Media Studies, Photography, Videography, Film Production, Animation, ICT, Telecommunications, Food & Beverage, Hotel Management, Housekeeping and Laundry management, Records Management, Records & Archives, Library & Information Science, Security & Safety, Security Management, Criminology, Criminal Justice, Forensics, Business Administration, Business Management, Project Management, Monitoring & Evaluation, Counselling Psychology, Occupational Health, Disaster Management, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Civil Engineering, Structural Engineering, Land Economics, Property Management, Quantity Survey, Actuarial Science, Transport & Logistics, Fleet management, Law, Education, Chemistry, Analytical Chemistry, Stenographers.</p> <h4>Requirements</h4> <ul> <li>Be a Kenyan Citizen aged below 35 years.</li> <li>Should be a <strong>continuing 1<sup>st</sup> degree student (Undergraduate)</strong> in their 3<sup>rd</sup>, 4<sup>th</sup> and final year of study and <strong>Diploma students </strong>in their final year from a recognised University/College.</li> <li>Should have a valid introduction letter from the University/College.</li> <li>Must be available full-time for the three-month duration of the program.</li> </ul> <h4>Terms & Gudielines</h4> <ul> <li>The deadline for the application is <strong>Friday, 6<sup>th</sup> March 2026</strong>.</li> <li>All applications must be submitted online via the process outlined below (<em>Application Guidelines).</em></li> <li>You can only apply for the opportunity after successfully registering on our e-recruitment portal.</li> <li>Incomplete applications will not be considered.</li> <li>The Authority does not extend the Attachment Program.</li> <li>ONLY selected candidates will be contacted.</li> <li><strong>The Authority does not charge any fee for this process</strong>.</li> <li>Monthly Stipend of <strong>KShs. 7,000 </strong>for undergraduates and diploma students will be paid and is subject to applicable statutory deductions.</li> <li>If selected, candidates will be required to submit proof of a valid Personal Accident Insurance Cover, copies of KRA PIN certificate, NHIF/SHIF, NSSF, ID card and Bank Account details.</li> </ul>

detail.overview: <p>Mwananchi Credit Limited is Kenya’s leading provider of Logbook Loans and other Secured Emergency Loans.</p> <p><strong>Job Title</strong>: Public Relations Intern</p> <p><strong>Vacancies:</strong> 1</p> <p><strong>Branch(s):</strong> Eco Bank Towers</p> <p><strong>Employment Type</strong>: Full-Time</p> <p><strong>Open Until</strong>: Feb 28, 2026</p> <p><strong>Role Overview</strong></p> <p>The PR Intern will support the Corporate Communications team in executing media relations, public relations, digital content, and internal communication initiatives. This role offers hands-on exposure to corporate reputation management, stakeholder engagement, and brand communication in a structured professional environment.</p> <h4>Duties and Responsibilities</h4> <p>1. Corporate Communications Support</p> <ul> <li>Assist in drafting press releases, newsletters, speeches, and corporate statements.</li> <li>Edit and proofread communication materials to ensure clarity and consistency.</li> <li>Support alignment of messaging across digital, internal, and external platforms.</li> <li>Maintain organized communication templates and brand guideline documents.</li> </ul> <p>2. Media Relations Support</p> <ul> <li>Maintain and update media contact databases.</li> <li>Assist in distributing press releases and media invitations.</li> <li>Monitor daily media coverage and compile brand mention reports.</li> <li>Support preparation of media kits and briefing documents.</li> </ul> <p>3. Public Relations & Reputation Support</p> <ul> <li>Monitor online mentions and escalate potential reputational concerns.</li> <li>Assist in drafting responses to general inquiries under supervision.</li> <li>Support execution of PR initiatives that enhance brand credibility.</li> </ul> <p>4. Digital & Content Support</p> <ul> <li>Assist in developing content for blogs, website updates, and social platforms.</li> <li>Support coordination of social media messaging aligned with PR objectives.</li> <li>Track and report engagement metrics for PR-related digital content.</li> </ul> <p>5. Internal Communications Support</p> <ul> <li>Assist in drafting internal memos and staff newsletters.</li> <li>Support HR communication for employee engagement initiatives.</li> <li>Help coordinate logistics for internal events and corporate activities.</li> </ul> <p>6. Stakeholder Engagement Support</p> <ul> <li>Assist in preparing communication materials for CSR activities and stakeholder forums.</li> <li>Support documentation and reporting for sponsorships and partnerships.</li> </ul> <p>7. Monitoring & Reporting</p> <ul> <li>Compile weekly and monthly media monitoring reports.</li> <li>Track basic PR metrics such as media mentions and estimated reach.</li> <li>Conduct competitor and industry communication research.</li> </ul> <p>8. Event Management Support</p> <ul> <li>Assist in planning and logistics for press conferences, launches, and CSR events.</li> <li>Support guest coordination and media follow-ups.</li> <li>Prepare post-event coverage reports and documentation.</li> </ul> <h4>Minimum Requirements</h4> <ul> <li>Diploma or Bachelor’s degree (ongoing or completed) in Communications, Journalism, Public Relations, Marketing, or a related field.</li> <li>Strong written and verbal communication skills in English.</li> <li>Basic understanding of media operations and corporate communication principles.</li> <li>Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).</li> <li>Basic knowledge of social media platforms and digital communication tools.</li> <li>Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.</li> <li>High level of professionalism and ability to handle confidential information.</li> </ul>

rawJobPosting.title: 2026 Primary Teacher Internship

detail.title: 2026 Primary Teacher Internship
detail.category: Early Childhood Development & Education; Education & Training
detail.overview: <p>Nova Pioneer Tatu International is looking for superstar educators who are currently enrolled or have recently graduated from a teaching college or university and would like to be selected <u><strong>unpaid/ teaching internship opportunity</strong> </u><strong>for three(3) months </strong>in<strong> </strong>an enquiry-based teaching model starting in January 2026. The volunteer teacher will be exposed to delivering an exceptional 21st Century learning experience for our students. <strong> </strong><br /> This is an exciting opportunity to be a part of an innovative network of schools that is developing generations of innovators and leaders who will shape the African Century.</p> <p>The benefits associated with the program will include the provision of meals while on campus and a letter of service upon successful completion of the program.</p> <h4>ABOUT NOVA PIONEER</h4> <p>Imagine a world in which every young person across Africa attends a school that truly sets them up for success in the 21st century. A school that cares about individual students and allows them to explore the world around them. A school that focuses more on developing skills than on memorizing content. At Nova Pioneer, we have done more than just imagine this future, we are bringing it to life. Nova Pioneer is a pan-African, independent network of schools offering high quality, accessible education from pre-primary through secondary school. Nova Pioneer uses a unique, inquiry-based learning approach that encourages students to ask “why” instead of telling them the “what”. It allows them to form their own thinking through exploration, investigation, and collaboration. Nova Pioneer currently has 14 schools in South Africa and Kenya with a vision to launch 100 schools and reach tens of thousands of students across the African continent in the next decade. To learn more visit<a href="http://www.novapioneer.com/"> www.novapioneer.com</a>.</p> <h4>ABOUT THE ROLE</h4> <p>Key responsibilities for the role include:</p> <ul> <li>Provide a safe and secure environment for children to feel comfortable.</li> <li>Plan and execute appropriate activities for the children each day according to the curriculum, schemes of work and lesson plans.</li> <li>Provide a variety of materials and resources for children to explore, manipulate, and use, both in learning activities and in imaginative play.</li> <li>Instruct and monitor students in the use and care of equipment and materials, in order to prevent injuries and damage.</li> <li>Organize and lead activities designed to promote physical, mental, and social development such as games.</li> <li>Prepare materials, classrooms, and other indoor and outdoor spaces to facilitate creative play, learning and motor-skill activities, and safety.</li> <li>Observe and evaluate each child’s progress and provide a written report to the Principal, other school leaders and parents.</li> <li>Monitor and keep records/reports of the children’s progress and development.</li> <li>Instruct students individually and in groups, adapting teaching methods to meet students’ varying needs and interests.</li> <li>Assimilate arriving children to the school environment by greeting them, helping them remove outerwear, and selecting activities of interest to them.</li> <li>Establish and enforce rules for behavior, and policies and procedures to maintain order among students.</li> <li>Plan and supervise class projects, field trips, visits by guests, or other experiential activities, and guide students in learning from those activities.</li> <li>Attend professional meetings, educational conferences, and teacher training workshops in order to maintain and improve professional competence.</li> </ul> <h4>ABOUT YOU</h4> <p><strong>Skills and Qualifications required:</strong></p> <ul> <li>You are currently enrolled or have completed a teacher training course - Bachelor of Education, Primary Teacher Education, ECDE, etc.</li> <li>You have strong recommendations from your high school, teacher training institution and/or an educational institution you have worked with.</li> <li>You aspire to join Nova Pioneer as an employee when the opportunity arises.</li> <li>You have a passion for children.</li> <li>You have good working skills with technology such as G-Suite, Zoom, Word, Powerpoint and Excel.</li> <li>You are a great communicator. You are able to provide clear and effective written and verbal communication.</li> <li>You are flexible and open to change - you handle uncertainty, complexity and ambiguity well.</li> <li>You are a problem solver - you are proactive in identifying issues and address these thoroughly by making an effort to find appropriate solutions.</li> <li>You are excited about our organisational culture—and in particular, you are fired up to challenge yourself by embracing mutual vulnerability and a firehose of feedback.</li> <li>You have a desire to further develop your leadership skills and apply them to transform the lives of African youth</li> </ul> <h4>WORKING AT NOVA PIONEER</h4> <p>Nova Pioneer is a place where our people experience tremendous growth, fast! We invest heavily in the growth of our people because it leads to greater mastery in both us and our students. “Novaneers” work harder and work longer hours than most schools but as a result, the growth they experience is extraordinary. We offer consistent and ongoing personal development and provide clear career pathways that ensure that our people are always achieving their personal and professional best. Nova Pioneer has a highly energetic, entrepreneurial environment where our people get to bring their creative and innovative ideas to life. We look for people who are flexible and who are able to adapt and respond quickly to adversity with a positive, can-do attitude (there are no problems, only solutions!). We welcome challenges and roll with the punches because it makes us, and our students, better. There is no problem too big for us! We bring infectious energy and enthusiasm to everything we do because joy fuels our work and learning. Read more about our culture principles<a href="http://novapioneer.com/kenya/learning/our-culture/"> here.</a></p>

detail.overview: <p>Join Britam as a <strong>Software Developer Attaché</strong> for a pivotal 3-month opportunity based in Nairobi, Kenya. This dynamic entry-level role challenges bright, aspiring technologists to spearhead the analysis, design, and implementation of cutting-edge digital solutions within the innovative Betalab environment. Successful candidates will deliver full-stack development, encompassing everything from robust database structures and efficient algorithms to intuitive user interfaces, directly impacting business efficiency and revenue generation. If you possess a strong technical foundation, familiarity with modern frameworks like React/Angular, and are eager to contribute clean, impactful code in a fast-paced setting, this is your chance to launch your career.</p> <hr /> <p><strong>Job Purpose:</strong></p> <p>Work within Betalab under the Partnership and Digital department to identify requirements, design and develop solutions (database design, workflows, user/data interfaces, integrations) and offer technical support for Betalab solutions.</p> <h4>Key responsibilities:</h4> <ul> <li>Champion the development of digital solutions by carrying out business analysis, designing end user testing documents, collaborating with internal and external parties to carry out user testing.</li> <li>Work with developers to design algorithms and flowcharts.</li> <li>Design and development of front-end tier(s), middle tier(s), and /or back-end database tier(s) for business applications.</li> <li>Perform the necessary technical design and development functionality to ensure that business application systems can be effectively developed and implemented.</li> <li>Enhance and create user and system documentation as needed.</li> <li>Perform data modelling to analyze and specify data structures within an application system.</li> <li>Developing database objects and structures for data storage, retrieval and reporting according to specifications.</li> <li>Produce clean, efficient code based on specifications.</li> <li>Integrate software components and third-party programs.</li> <li>Verify and deploy programs and systems.</li> <li>Troubleshoot, debug and upgrade existing software.</li> <li>Gather and evaluate user feedback.</li> <li>Capturing of business applications information needs and mapping of the same to the software and /or database components.</li> <li>Research and provide input on design approach, performance and base functionality improvements for various procedures and applications.</li> <li>Generation of ideas to improve efficiency in software and application services offered to the business, and/or generate revenue for business.</li> </ul> <h4>Knowledge, experience, and qualifications required:</h4> <ul> <li>Degree in Computer Science or technical-related field from an accredited institution.</li> <li>Familiarity with Agile development methodologies.</li> <li>Development experience with any modern programming tools/Platforms e.g. .NET, Java, Ruby, PHP.</li> <li>Development experience with Spring boot and React or Angular Framework.</li> <li>Certified Solution Developer/ Architect e.g. Java, Microsoft is an added advantage.</li> <li>Experience in applications development, which includes system customization and report designs.</li> <li>Experience with RDMS preferably MS SQL Server, Oracle, MySQL, Sybase, and Informix.</li> </ul>

KWAL is a HEINEKEN OpCo.</p> <p><strong>Customer Service & Logistics Intern</strong></p> <p><strong>Function</strong>: Supply Chain<br /> <strong>Sub Function</strong>: Supply Chain - Customer, Logistics and Distribution<br /> <strong>Type of Contract</strong>: Fixed Term</p> <p>Are you obsessed with smart operations, data, and delivering top‑tier customer experiences? detail.overview: <p>Ready to put your communication skills to work for a better world? <strong>Apply now for WeWorld’s Communications Internship in Nairobi!</strong> Help us amplify the voices of the communities we serve and drive positive change in Kenya. This is your chance to gain hands-on experience in international development communications and contribute to a meaningful mission.</p> <hr /> <p>WeWorld is an independent Italian organization working in the field of Development Cooperation and Humanitarian Aid projects in 27 countries. We work to promote recognition and respect of the fundamental rights of every human being, fighting poverty, injustice, and generating sustainable and durable socio-economic growth models. We strive for a better world where everyone, especially women and children, have equal opportunities and rights, access to resources, to health, to education and to dignified work. A world in which the environment is a common good to be respected and preserved; in which war, violence and exploitation are banned. We feel called upon to increase our capabilities of providing support, in a global context characterized by economic and environmental crises, conflicts and inequalities, in which Humanitarian Aid and Development Cooperation are more linked than ever.</p> <p>WeWorld has been operating in Kenya since 2009 implementing projects in the counties of Isiolo, Nairobi, Narok, Kwale, Migori. Our key thematic areas of intervention focus on: Health and Nutrition, Water Sanitation, Food security, Resilience, Climate Change and Climate Action, Livelihood, peacebuilding, Education, Socio-Economic Development, Capacity Strengthening of local CSOs, and Youth empowerment and youth meaningful participation, Gender issues, Child protection and environmental sustainability are mainstreamed in all our actions.</p> <p><strong>Job Title</strong>: Communications Intern - Nairobi (1 Position)<br /> <strong>Reports to</strong>: Communication, Advocacy & Fundraising Coordinator<br /> <strong>Job Location</strong>: Nairobi, Kenya with possible missions to field offices<br /> <strong>Contract Details</strong>: 6-month Internship (renewable subject to performance and funding availability)</p> <h4>Job Duties and Responsibilities</h4> <p>Under the line management of the Communication, Advocacy & Fundraising Coordinator, the Communications Intern will support the implementation of communication, advocacy, and visibility activities. In particular, the intern will perform the following tasks:</p> <p><strong>1. Content Development & Digital Communication</strong></p> <ul> <li>Support the creation of communication materials (articles, success stories, social media posts, newsletters, and infographics).</li> <li>Assist in managing the Kenya content calendar and LinkedIn page.</li> <li>Support implementation of digital campaigns aligned with project objectives.</li> <li>Assist in drafting website content in coordination with HQ Communications team.</li> </ul> <p><strong>2. Documentation & Media Support</strong></p> <ul> <li>Support photo and video documentation in the field, ensuring proper consent procedures are followed.</li> <li>Organize and archive photos, videos, and communication materials on SharePoint in line with WeWorld guidelines.</li> <li>Prepare IEC and visibility materials for projects.</li> </ul> <p><strong>3. Advocacy & Events Support</strong></p> <ul> <li>Support planning and implementation of awareness campaigns, workshops, and public events.</li> <li>Assist in documenting key project milestones, lessons learned, and best practices.</li> <li>Ensure brand compliance in all communication outputs.</li> </ul> <p><strong>4. Reporting & Coordination</strong></p> <ul> <li>Assist in preparing internal communication reports and activity summaries.</li> <li>Support coordination with HQ communications focal points when required.</li> <li>Perform any other communication-related tasks assigned by the supervisor or country director.</li> </ul> <h4>Minimum Education, Qualifications & Experience Required</h4> <p><strong>Education</strong></p> <ul> <li>Recently completed a Diploma or Degree in Communications, Journalism, Public Relations, Advertising, or related field.</li> <li>Computer literacy (Microsoft Office, SharePoint, social media platforms).</li> </ul> <p><strong>Experience</strong></p> <ul> <li>Previous internship or volunteer experience in communications, media, or NGOs is an added advantage.</li> <li>Experience in content creation, or social media management is desirable.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Technical Skills and Knowledge</strong></p> <ul> <li>Strong written and verbal communication skills in English and Swahili.</li> <li>Basic graphic design skills (e.g., Canva or Adobe tools).</li> <li>Creativity and attention to detail.</li> <li>Strong organizational skills and ability to meet deadlines.</li> <li>Cultural sensitivity and ability to work in a multicultural environment.</li> <li>Basic photography and videography skills.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Desirable Qualifications</strong></p> <ul> <li>Additional Experience</li> <li>Experience working with INGOs or community-based organizations.</li> <li>Interest in gender equality, child protection, and development communication.</li> <li>Understanding branding and digital engagement trends.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Personal Attributes</strong></p> <ul> <li>High level of integrity and professionalism.</li> <li>Willingness to learn and take initiative.</li> <li>Ability to work both independently and in a team.</li> </ul>

detail.title: Innovation Internship
detail.category: Data Science; Information Systems; International Relations/Development; Public Policy / Administration; Social Sciences
detail.overview: <p><strong>Ready to make a real difference for children worldwide? Apply now for the Innovation Intern position at Save the Children International and be a part of driving impactful solutions!</strong> If you are a curious, proactive individual with a passion for making a difference and a keen interest in leveraging new technologies to solve complex problems, we encourage you to apply and join our dynamic Innovation Team. This role provides hands-on experience in developing, testing, and scaling innovative solutions, offering valuable experience and contributing to lasting impact for children around the world.</p> <hr /> <p>Save the Children is the world’s leading organisation for children, employing ~25,000 staff. We save children’s lives. We fight for their rights. We help them fulfil their potential. Through our work in 116 countries, we put the most deprived and marginalised children first.</p> <p>Save the Children International has an exciting opportunity for a <strong>Innovation Intern</strong> to join our global team.</p> <h4>About the Team</h4> <p>Innovation is at the heart of Save the Children’s theory of change for how we will drive impact with and for children. Our team inspires and supports innovation across Save the Children with the objective to use new ideas, emerging technologies, and different ways of working to address complex problems and amplify our impact for children. The team is focused on accelerating innovation through:</p> <ul> <li>Adding fuel – driving initiatives that create a culture and practice of innovation across Save the Children</li> <li>Reducing Friction – building internal ways of working and systems that inspire and enable innovators</li> <li>Clear for Launch – discovering and scaling high potential solutions from both within and external to Save the Children</li> </ul> <h4>Role purpose</h4> <p>The Innovation Intern plays a hands-on role in developing, testing, and scaling of innovative solutions. As part of the Innovation Team’s Strategic Plan, the intern will help:</p> <ul> <li>Identify promising innovation solutions and opportunities.</li> <li>Support the mapping and development of fundraising and partnership opportunities.</li> <li>Build innovation capability across teams and country offices.</li> <li>Apply artificial intelligence and digital tools to increase the impact and efficiency of the Innovation Team’s work.</li> </ul> <p>This role is designed for someone who is curious, proactive, and excited by experimentation. You bring fresh thinking and have a track record of making a positive difference whether in your community, studies, or work. You are adaptable, motivated by impact, and enjoy learning by doing, collaborating with others, and using new tools and technologies to solve real-world problems.</p> <p><strong>Job Title:</strong> Innovation Intern<br /> <strong>Work Pattern:</strong> Hybrid/Remote with flexible working options available<br /> <strong>Contract Length:</strong> 3 months<br /> <strong>Location:</strong> Any approved Save the Children International office location. For a full list of locations that Save the Children International can hire in, please visit: <a href="https://www.savethechildren.net/careers" target="_blank" rel="nofollow noopener">SCI Careers</a><br /> <strong>Time Zone </strong>(<i>that the role holder <strong>must </strong>be available to work in</i>)<strong>: </strong>Any<br /> <strong>Right to Work:</strong> The successful candidate must possess the unrestricted right to work in their current or preferred location for the duration of employment.<br /> <strong>Language Requirements: </strong>English</p> <h4>Principal Accountabilities</h4> <p>The intern will support key innovation priorities across a range of workstreams, which may include:</p> <p><strong>Open Innovation Challenge</strong></p> <ul> <li>Support the design and launch of an open innovation challenge to identify and source high-potential solutions aligned with organisational priorities.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Donor Landscape Mapping for Innovation Funding</strong></p> <ul> <li>Research and map key innovation funders, including decision-makers, funding priorities, recent investments, and potential entry points for engagement.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Innovation Pipeline Research</strong></p> <ul> <li>Conduct structured desk research to identify and catalogue 50+ promising innovations relevant to Save the Children’s work.</li> <li>Produce a shortlist of high-potential innovations, with concise assessment summaries to inform decision-making.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Innovation Astronaut Program</strong></p> <ul> <li>Support the design and roll-out of the Innovation Astronaut Program.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Innovation Team AI-Enabled Workflows</strong></p> <ul> <li>Weekly Innovation Trends Newsletter: Develop and produce a weekly internal newsletter summarising emerging trends, tools, sector developments, and opportunities to support the innovation team’s situational awareness.</li> <li>Automated Monthly New Business Development Email: Design and implement an automated system to generate monthly emails highlighting relevant funding opportunities.</li> <li>AI Funding Opportunity Bot: Enhance an existing AI-based tool to improve accuracy, broaden data sources, and introduce additional features such as deadline tracking and relevance scoring.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Business as usual</strong></p> <ul> <li>The intern may support additional innovation tasks as defined by the Innovation team.</li> </ul> <h4>Experience and Skills</h4> <p><strong>Essential</strong></p> <p>1<i><strong>.</strong></i>Research and Analysis</p> <ul> <li>Strong desk-research skills, including the ability to identify, review, and synthesise information from multiple sources.</li> <li>Ability to assess innovations, funding opportunities, and tools and summarise findings clearly.</li> </ul> <p>2.Written and Verbal Communication</p> <ul> <li>Clear, concise writing skills, particularly for briefs, summaries, and internal communications.</li> <li>Ability to present ideas and findings in a structured and accessible way.</li> </ul> <p>3.Digital and AI Literacy</p> <ul> <li>Comfortable using digital tools to support research, collaboration, and workflow management.</li> <li>Familiarity with AI-enabled tools for research, summarisation, or automation, or strong willingness to learn.</li> </ul> <p>4.Project and Task Management</p> <ul> <li>Ability to manage multiple tasks, prioritise effectively, and meet deadlines.</li> <li>Basic planning and coordination skills to support innovation challenges and research activities.</li> </ul> <h4>Education and Qualifications</h4> <p><strong>Essential</strong></p> <ul> <li>Recently completed an undergraduate degree in a relevant field, such as: <ul> <li>Design, innovation, or business</li> <li>Data, information systems, or related analytical disciplines</li> <li>International development, public policy, or social sciences</li> </ul> </li> <li>Other relevant fields with demonstrated interest in innovation and social impact</li> <li>Equivalent practical experience, coursework, or demonstrated capability will be considered in place of formal qualifications.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Desirable</strong></p> <ul> <li>Project work that shows initiative , personal projects, hackathons, case competitions, or research that demonstrates they can take an idea from concept to execution</li> <li>Exposure to design thinking or similar innovation frameworks</li> <li>Experience with prototyping or experimentation (could be digital tools, physical making, or process design)</li> <li>Cross-functional work - internships or projects that required working with different departments or disciplines</li> <li>Any involvement with start-ups, incubators, or entrepreneurship programs.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Working at Save the Children International</strong></p> <p>We know that great people make a great organisation, and that our employees play a crucial role in helping us achieve our ambitions for children. We value our people and offer a meaningful and rewarding career, along with a collaborative and inclusive environment where ambition, creativity, and integrity are highly valued.</p> <p>The work here is challenging but is also immensely rewarding. At Save the Children, you will be in good company, working with talented, like-minded individuals who are determined to ensure that all children survive, learn, and are protected. Your contribution will help ensure children’s voices are heard at the highest levels, and that we achieve our global strategy, <strong>Ambition for Children 2030</strong>, and reach every last child.</p>

resilience</li> <li>Accountability and initiative</li> <li>Process improvement mindset</li> <li>Strong verbal and written communication</li> </ol> <h4>Academic Qualifications and Relevant Come grow your career mastering essential operational technology within a vital logistics sector.</p> <p>Bulkstream owns and operates world-class bulk terminals in the port of Mombasa, designed for the handling of 8 million metric tonnes of dry and liquid bulk commodities per year across the East African corridor through inland terminals, to distribution centres and warehouses.</p> <p>This role will manage and support ICT infrastructure. This entails installation, management, operation, and maintenance of business systems hardware and software. The role ensures that all deployed ICT systems and infrastructure are working well for all staff.</p> <h4>KEY RESPONSIBILITIES</h4> <ol> <li>Analyzing existing systems and offering new ideas for improvement.</li> <li>Participation in the development and implementation of ICT policy and best practice guidelines for the department</li> <li>Ensure ICT policies and procedures of the company are enforced</li> <li>Participating effectively in major ICT projects including system updates, upgrades, migrations and outages.</li> <li>Identify evolving staff ICT needs and recommend necessary actions.</li> <li>Assign configuration of authentication and authorization of directory services.</li> <li>Review daily backup operations to ensure they are done as per policy and procedures</li> <li>Manage assets, inventory, labeling and filing for end user computers and peripherals</li> <li>Run Health checks, hardware diagnostics and optimization on computers and peripheral devices such as printers, copiers and scanners to maintain optimum hardware and software performance</li> <li>Maintain and support desktops/laptops Windows applications (MS Office Suite, Management software, accounting software), install vendor Critical and Security patches and updates</li> <li>Support computer users to ensure that all computers, software and peripherals are functioning properly including maintenances and upgrades required</li> <li>Ensuring strong alignment to, and application of, ITIL based service management processes such as Incident, Change, Problem, Event, Release and Capacity Management.</li> <li>Investigate, troubleshoot, and resolve issues reported by staff on computing and ICT services</li> <li>Manage repair and recovery from hardware or software failures. Review and manage ongoing performance tuning, hardware upgrades, and resource optimization as required.</li> <li>Work with other business units and external clients to resolve ICT issues</li> <li>Providing management reports on weekly basis</li> </ol> <h4>ACADEMIC REQUIREMENTS</h4> <ol> <li>Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in ICT and engineering disciplines such as Computer Science, Information Technology, electrical/electronic/software/telecommunications engineering</li> <li>Relevant professional certifications.</li> </ol> <p><strong>SKILLS & COMPETENCIES</strong></p> <ul> <li>Multi-tasking skills</li> <li>Positive attitude with a growth mindset</li> <li>Good writing and verbal communication skills</li> <li>Research, Technological awareness and data analysis skills</li> <li>Interpersonal Skills</li> <li>Attentive to details</li> <li>Highly organized</li> <li>Passionate on service</li> </ul> <h4>KEY PERFORMANCE INDICATORS</h4> <ul> <li>Accurate and timely reporting on ticketing system</li> <li>Excellent employee NPS on ICT services offered</li> <li>Timely and effective tasks completion</li> <li>Demonstration of skills and knowledge growth</li> </ul>
